Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Datahäxan 112 upvotes, 9 comments
  2. Printf-Tac-Toe 98 upvotes, 9 comments
  3. Dolphin Progress Release 2603 214 upvotes, 30 comments
  4. High fidelity font synthesis for CJK languages 35 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Avoiding Trigonometry (2013) 167 upvotes, 39 comments
  6. Italian prosecutors seek trial for Amazon, 4 execs in alleged $1.4B tax evasion 62 upvotes, 12 comments
  7. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans 4014 upvotes, 1505 comments
  8. The Met Releases High-Def 3D Scans of 140 Famous Art Objects 51 upvotes, 11 comments
  9. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 386 upvotes, 130 comments
  10. Tested: How Many Times Can a DVD±RW Be Rewritten? Methodology and Results 222 upvotes, 72 comments
  11. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 184 upvotes, 63 comments
  12. Reliable Software in the LLM Era 71 upvotes, 21 comments
  13. Atlassian CEO: AI doesn't replace people here, but we're firing them anyway 63 upvotes, 18 comments
  14. Claude now creates interactive charts, diagrams and visualizations 28 upvotes, 6 comments
  15. The Cost of Indirection in Rust 23 upvotes, 5 comments
  16. Asia rolls out 4-day weeks, WFH to solve fuel crisis caused by Iran war 132 upvotes, 55 comments
  17. ArcaOS 5.1.2 (based on OS/2 Warp 4.52) now available 38 upvotes, 14 comments
  18. SHOW HN: A usage circuit breaker for Cloudflare Workers 24 upvotes, 8 comments
  19. Emacs internals: Tagged pointers vs. C++ std:variant and LLVM (Part 3) 38 upvotes, 16 comments
  20. Returning to Rails in 2026 292 upvotes, 186 comments
  21. US banks' exposure to private credit hits $300B (2025) 151 upvotes, 93 comments
  22. Show HN: We analyzed 1,573 Claude Code sessions to see how AI agents work 92 upvotes, 56 comments
  23. SBCL: A Sanely-Bootstrappable Common Lisp (2008) [pdf] 102 upvotes, 68 comments
  24. Big Data on the Cheapest MacBook 240 upvotes, 207 comments
  25. Kotlin creator's new language: a formal way to talk to LLMs instead of English 123 upvotes, 104 comments
  26. Colon cancer now leading cause of cancer deaths under 50 in US 96 upvotes, 97 comments
  27. Suburban school district uses license plate readers to verify student residency 122 upvotes, 150 comments
  28. ATMs didn't kill bank Teller jobs, but the iPhone did 112 upvotes, 144 comments
  29. Show HN: OneCLI – Vault for AI Agents in Rust 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Hive (YC S14) is hiring scrappy product managers and product/data engineers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 13 March 2026. Source here.
